inline_comments.message: This string is used as pattern for grep in line 47 so "[" and "]" should be escaped, otherwise it won't work as grep will treat is as bracket expression. Please test the changes before pushing :)

I would recommend not creating tests that are dependent on internal information that are inherently highly subject to change, like log messages, internal configuration files (/etc/supervisord.conf, /etc/supervisord.d/*), and so on.
The project provides tox-based tests that I tried, without success, to integrate into repo, using JJB (see https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/103588).
It performs functional black-box testing of the container using ncclient as an independent NETCONF client.

Totally agree. Verify procedure I came up with was the consequence of lack of native working application level tests embedded within repo that could be used here to verify the service status. Should you provide those in the project files I'll be happy to update this job to leverage them. 

In fact, if I'm not mistaken, there are app level tests in csit repository but that's not the deal here - in integration project where only docker services bootstrapping code lives the only thing that's need verifying is whether the docker infra was brought up correctly without issues.

Good.
But this project in particular doesn't create an application and then packages it in a docker image. Thus, a container launched from this image is the only artifact to be tested.
My previous attempt was to only run

> tox

after the image building.
